channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lawyers Boycott Court Hearings Over Judicial Disciplinary Issues
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has directed all advocates across the country to skip court hearings with immediate effect, citing a protest against judicial disciplinary actions. The directive include...

item.title: Commentator Argues Backlash Against Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' Failed as Film Opens Strong
item.description: The article reports on commentary by media critic Brian Lowry, who argues that Elon Musk and Donald Trump Jr.'s public criticism of Christopher Nolan's film 'The Odyssey' ultimately failed to influenc...

item.title: Mount Olympus seeks UNESCO World Heritage status for cultural and natural significance
item.description: Mount Olympus, the mythical home of ancient Greece's 12 Olympian gods, is being considered for UNESCO World Heritage status as a mixed cultural and natural site. The nomination, to be discussed at the...

item.title: Tanzania and Egypt finalize plans for Dar-Cairo cargo transport hub
item.description: Tanzania and Egypt are finalizing plans to establish a strategic cargo transport hub linking the Port of Dar es Salaam with Egypt’s Ain Sokhna Port. This initiative aims to create a maritime logistics...

item.title: Delta Air Lines Launches Nonstop Route Between Los Angeles and Manila in 2027, Boosting Travel Options
item.description: Delta Air Lines, a US-based airline, is set to launch a nonstop flight route between Los Angeles (LAX) and Manila (NAIA) starting March 28, 2027. This service will initially operate three times a week...

item.title: East Rutherford Hosts World Cup Infrastructure Amid Municipal Collaboration
item.description: The article discusses the efforts of small city mayors to construct stadiums in East Rutherford as part of the World Cup preparations. It highlights the collaboration between local governments and the...

item.title: Suspect in Ann Widdecombe's murder arrested after investigation into alleged terrorist attack
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former Tory minister and MEP, was found dead in her Devon home on July 8, 2026. The 78-year-old politician is suspected of being killed in a terrorist attack by Joshua Kerry, a 28-ye...

item.title: Lagos Housing Units Forfeited to Federal Government Over Illegal Activities
item.description: The Federal High Court in Lagos has ordered the final forfeiture of 52 residential properties in Lagos State to the federal government, citing evidence that these assets were acquired through illicit ...

item.title: Man convicted of sexual assault receives prison sentence
item.description: A man has been convicted of sexual assault and sentenced to prison after a court found him guilty of the crime. The case, which involved a victim's testimony and forensic evidence, concluded with the ...

item.title: Inner Melbourne weather forecast for today
item.description: The article discusses the weather conditions expected in Inner Melbourne today. It includes details about potential temperature ranges, rainfall probabilities, and wind speeds. The text also contains ...

item.title: Lok Sabha Speaker Approves Merger of Uddhav Sena MPs with Shinde Faction, Amid Political Realignments
item.description: The Lok Sabha Speaker, Om Birla, has approved the merger of six rebel MPs from the Shiv Sena (Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray) faction with the rival Eknath Shinde-led faction. These six MPs, part of the U...

item.title: IDAC Ombudsman to Address Controversies Over Corruption Allegations
item.description: The Independent Directorate Against Corruption (IDAC) Ombudsman, led by Retired Judge Takalani Raulinga, will address media concerns regarding IDAC's controversies following revelations at the Madlang...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Threatens World Cup Final in New Jersey Amid Health Concerns
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canada has severely impacted air quality across the eastern United States, creating a hazardous environment for the upcoming World Cup final in New Jersey. The haze, which has blan...

item.title: Nova Scotia emergency medical dispatchers go on strike, affecting critical services
item.description: Over 60 emergency medical dispatchers in Nova Scotia are currently on strike, disrupting essential emergency response services across the province. The walkout, which began on July 18, 2026, has raise...

item.title: Siaya Governor James Orengo Demands Inclusion in Linda Mwananchi's Opposition Talks
item.description: Siaya Governor James Orengo has publicly stated he must be part of any negotiations involving Linda Mwananchi and the United Opposition for the 2027 presidential race. Orengo cited his role in the 200...

item.title: Governor Mutfwang Pushes for Private Sector Leadership in Plateau Industrial Growth
item.description: The Governor of Plateau State, Caleb Mutfwang, has committed to building a private sector-driven economy by focusing on manufacturing, agriculture, and infrastructure. During a breakfast meeting with ...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens efforts to fight human trafficking
item.description: Tanzania has intensified its strategies to combat human trafficking, a critical issue affecting the nation's security and development. The government has announced new measures aimed at preventing the...

item.title: Venezuela's Earthquake Death Toll Exceeds 5,000
item.description: The death toll from two powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela in June 2026 has risen to 5,069, according to local authorities. The interim government of President Delcy Rodriguez reported that 16...

item.title: Palace Refutes Cayetano's Allegation of Marcos Rejecting ₱10K Aid
item.description: The Philippine Palace has strongly denied the claim made by Senator Alan Peter Cayetano regarding former President Ferdinand Marcos' alleged rejection of a ₱10,000 aid package. In a statement released...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Leadership Challenges as New Labour Prime Minister
item.description: Andy Burnham's ascension to Prime Minister marks a pivotal moment for the Labour Party, which faces internal divisions and external pressures. The article explores his leadership style, shaped by year...
